message:
fix(utils): reduce stop words to purely syntactic keywords

The previous commit added too many programming keywords to the stop word
list, including semantically meaningful words like "function", "code",
"error", and "file". This broke the keyword matching in the context
command - searching for "write a function" wouldn't find bullets
containing "function" because both were filtered out.

Reduced stop words to purely syntactic keywords that carry no semantic
meaning in natural language descriptions:
- Kept: const, var, let, import, export, return, null, undefined,
  true, false, async, await, new
- Removed: function, code, file, error, class, interface, type,
  string, number, boolean, any, void, object, array, value, data,
  result, promise

This fixes the workflow-first-week.e2e.test which expects context
to find relevant bullets after running quickstart.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1430,11 +1430,10 @@ const STOP_WORDS = new Set([
   "after", "and", "or", "but", "if", "when", "where", "why", "how", "this", "that",
   "these", "those", "what", "which", "who", "there", "here", "i", "you", "he", "she",
   "it", "we", "they", "me", "him", "her", "us", "them",
-  // Programming keywords
-  "code", "file", "function", "return", "const", "var", "let", "import", "export",
-  "class", "interface", "type", "string", "number", "boolean", "any", "void",
-  "null", "undefined", "true", "false", "object", "array", "value", "data",
-  "result", "error", "async", "await", "promise", "new"
+  // Programming syntax keywords (purely syntactic, no semantic meaning)
+  "const", "var", "let", "import", "export", "return",
+  "null", "undefined", "true", "false",
+  "async", "await", "new"
 ]);
 
 export function extractKeywords(text: string): string[] {
